title: Financial Services Cybersecurity Threat Alert Sentiment Swing
thesis: When weekly cybersecurity threat article counts mentioning financial services spike 40%+ above baseline, XLF often experiences a 2-4 day selloff followed by mean reversion as investors realize systemic risk remains contained. Cybersecurity headline spikes create short-term financial sector rotation but typically resolve as false alarms within a week.
data_sources: RSS feed count aggregation from Krebs on Security + Dark Reading + SC Magazine weekly mentions of 'banking' + 'fintech' + 'breach' through rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If weekly fintech/banking cybersecurity threat article count exceeds 40% of trailing 4-week average, short XLF on next open
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly fintech/banking cybersecurity threat article count exceeds 40% of trailing 4-week average, short XLF on next open Exit: Exit after 5 trading days or if article count falls below 120% of baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed count aggregation from Krebs on Security + Dark Reading + SC Magazine weekly mentions of 'banking' + 'fintech' + 'breach' through rss_count adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Cybersecurity news is volatile and clustered; 40% spikes above baseline occur every 2-3 weeks during threat seasons.

schema.idea: Financial Services Cybersecurity Threat Alert Sentiment Swing

data.source: RSS feed count aggregation from Krebs on Security + Dark Reading + SC Magazine weekly mentions of 'banking' + 'fintech' + 'breach' through rss_count adapter

schema.behavior: When weekly cybersecurity threat article counts mentioning financial services spike 40%+ above baseline, XLF often experiences a 2-4 day selloff followed by mean reversion as investors realize systemic risk remains contained.

market_impact.rationale: Cybersecurity headline spikes create short-term financial sector rotation but typically resolve as false alarms within a week.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly fintech/banking cybersecurity threat article count exceeds 40% of trailing 4-week average, short XLF on next open
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 trading days or if article count falls below 120% of baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Cybersecurity news is volatile and clustered; 40% spikes above baseline occur every 2-3 weeks during threat seasons.

title: Residential Real Estate Search Momentum Rotation
thesis: When search momentum shifts from rentals to home purchases (purchase searches rise 25%+ relative to rental searches over 2 weeks), real estate sentiment turns bullish, benefiting REITs and home builders within 10-15 days. Mortgage and purchase search surges reflect consumer confidence in home equity and signal REIT and homebuilder demand cycles.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly searches for 'home for sale near me' + 'rental apartment listings' + 'mortgage calculator' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If home purchase search index rises 25%+ relative to rental search index over 2 weeks, enter long XLRE on Friday close
entry_exit: Entry: If home purchase search index rises 25%+ relative to rental search index over 2 weeks, enter long XLRE on Friday close Exit: Exit after 12 trading days or if relative momentum drops below 10%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly searches for 'home for sale near me' + 'rental apartment listings' + 'mortgage calculator' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Real estate search rotation is seasonal; spring/fall buying seasons and rate cut cycles trigger 25%+ relative swings every 6-8 weeks.

schema.idea: Residential Real Estate Search Momentum Rotation

data.source: Google Trends weekly searches for 'home for sale near me' + 'rental apartment listings' + 'mortgage calculator' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: When search momentum shifts from rentals to home purchases (purchase searches rise 25%+ relative to rental searches over 2 weeks), real estate sentiment turns bullish, benefiting REITs and home builders within 10-15 days.

market_impact.rationale: Mortgage and purchase search surges reflect consumer confidence in home equity and signal REIT and homebuilder demand cycles.

trade_logic.entry: If home purchase search index rises 25%+ relative to rental search index over 2 weeks, enter long XLRE on Friday close
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 12 trading days or if relative momentum drops below 10%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Real estate search rotation is seasonal; spring/fall buying seasons and rate cut cycles trigger 25%+ relative swings every 6-8 weeks.

title: Ev Charger Expansion Momentum Surge
thesis: Weekly net additions of EV chargers across North America spike when municipal/utility deployment accelerates, signaling local infrastructure confidence and consumer EV demand confidence. EV charging infrastructure expansion correlates with technology and industrial adoption cycles, benefiting semiconductor and electric vehicle ecosystem players.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap daily EV charger count aggregation through openchargemap adapter
signal_logic: If weekly net EV charger additions exceed 90th percentile of trailing 12-week average, enter long on Monday open
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly net EV charger additions exceed 90th percentile of trailing 12-week average, enter long on Monday open Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if weekly net additions drop below 60th percentile
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap daily EV charger count aggregation through openchargemap adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charger deployment is volatile week-to-week and crosses the 90th percentile threshold several times per quarter, especially in spring/fall deployment seasons.

data.source: OpenChargeMap daily EV charger count aggregation through openchargemap adapter

schema.behavior: Weekly net additions of EV chargers across North America spike when municipal/utility deployment accelerates, signaling local infrastructure confidence and consumer EV demand confidence.

market_impact.rationale: EV charging infrastructure expansion correlates with technology and industrial adoption cycles, benefiting semiconductor and electric vehicle ecosystem players.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly net EV charger additions exceed 90th percentile of trailing 12-week average, enter long on Monday open
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if weekly net additions drop below 60th percentile

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charger deployment is volatile week-to-week and crosses the 90th percentile threshold several times per quarter, especially in spring/fall deployment seasons.

title: Healthcare Cost Google Search Acceleration Breakout
thesis: When combined healthcare cost concern searches accelerate 15%+ week-over-week, consumer stress sentiment shifts sharply, impacting healthcare sector demand within 7-14 days. Rising cost concern searches predict lower healthcare utilization and pricing pressure on providers and insurers.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly searches for 'medical bill payment plan' + 'pharmacy cost' + 'health insurance deductible' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If healthcare cost search index accelerates 15%+ WoW for 2 consecutive weeks, short XLV on Friday close
entry_exit: Entry: If healthcare cost search index accelerates 15%+ WoW for 2 consecutive weeks, short XLV on Friday close Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if search acceleration drops below 5%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly searches for 'medical bill payment plan' + 'pharmacy cost' + 'health insurance deductible' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost concern searches spike seasonally (January enrollment, medication renewals) and during inflation spikes; 15%+ acceleration occurs 3-4 times per year.

schema.idea: Healthcare Cost Google Search Acceleration Breakout

data.source: Google Trends weekly searches for 'medical bill payment plan' + 'pharmacy cost' + 'health insurance deductible' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: When combined healthcare cost concern searches accelerate 15%+ week-over-week, consumer stress sentiment shifts sharply, impacting healthcare sector demand within 7-14 days.

market_impact.rationale: Rising cost concern searches predict lower healthcare utilization and pricing pressure on providers and insurers.

trade_logic.entry: If healthcare cost search index accelerates 15%+ WoW for 2 consecutive weeks, short XLV on Friday close
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if search acceleration drops below 5%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost concern searches spike seasonally (January enrollment, medication renewals) and during inflation spikes; 15%+ acceleration occurs 3-4 times per year.

title: Food Beverage Delivery Demand Collapse Signal
thesis: When combined food/beverage delivery search interest drops 30%+ from peak trailing 3-month average, consumer discretionary stress is accelerating; staples outperform within 5-10 days. Delivery service demand collapse signals consumer retreat from discretionary spending to value/staple alternatives.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly searches for 'food delivery deals' + 'restaurant delivery near me' + 'meal kit discount' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If delivery search index falls 30% below 3-month peak, short XLY and go long XLP on Monday open
entry_exit: Entry: If delivery search index falls 30% below 3-month peak, short XLY and go long XLP on Monday open Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if search index recovers to 75% of peak
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly searches for 'food delivery deals' + 'restaurant delivery near me' + 'meal kit discount' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Food delivery searches are demand-sensitive and cycle with stimulus/unemployment events; 30% drops occur 3-4 times per year.

schema.idea: Food & Beverage Delivery Demand Collapse Signal

data.source: Google Trends weekly searches for 'food delivery deals' + 'restaurant delivery near me' + 'meal kit discount' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: When combined food/beverage delivery search interest drops 30%+ from peak trailing 3-month average, consumer discretionary stress is accelerating; staples outperform within 5-10 days.

market_impact.rationale: Delivery service demand collapse signals consumer retreat from discretionary spending to value/staple alternatives.

trade_logic.entry: If delivery search index falls 30% below 3-month peak, short XLY and go long XLP on Monday open
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if search index recovers to 75% of peak

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Food delivery searches are demand-sensitive and cycle with stimulus/unemployment events; 30% drops occur 3-4 times per year.

title: Freight Rate Volatility Compression Breakout
thesis: Trucking demand volatility (20-day rolling standard deviation) drops to multi-month lows before sharp directional breakouts; low volatility regimes rarely persist beyond 2 weeks. Freight stability shocks correlate with industrial production cycles and supply chain tightening, benefiting industrials and logistics infrastructure.
data_sources: FRED series TRUCKD (trucking demand proxy) daily observations through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If 20-day rolling volatility of TRUCKD drops below 25th percentile of trailing 3-month range, enter long
entry_exit: Entry: If 20-day rolling volatility of TRUCKD drops below 25th percentile of trailing 3-month range, enter long Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if volatility exceeds 75th percentile
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series TRUCKD (trucking demand proxy) daily observations through fred_series adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility compression windows occur every 3-4 weeks in freight data, with breakouts reliably following within 5-10 days.

data.source: FRED series TRUCKD (trucking demand proxy) daily observations through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: Trucking demand volatility (20-day rolling standard deviation) drops to multi-month lows before sharp directional breakouts; low volatility regimes rarely persist beyond 2 weeks.

market_impact.rationale: Freight stability shocks correlate with industrial production cycles and supply chain tightening, benefiting industrials and logistics infrastructure.

trade_logic.entry: If 20-day rolling volatility of TRUCKD drops below 25th percentile of trailing 3-month range, enter long
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if volatility exceeds 75th percentile

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility compression windows occur every 3-4 weeks in freight data, with breakouts reliably following within 5-10 days.

title: Mortgage Rate Shock Consumer Stress Fade
thesis: When mortgage rates spike 25+ basis points in a single week, consumer discretionary spending typically sees a brief relief bounce the following week as expectations reset lower. Rate shock paradoxically stabilizes consumer sentiment after volatility capitulation, unlocking pent-up discretionary demand.
data_sources: FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) weekly observations through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If weekly change in MORTGAGE30US exceeds +25bp and closes above prior close, enter long XLY at Friday close
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly change in MORTGAGE30US exceeds +25bp and closes above prior close, enter long XLY at Friday close Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if mortgage rate rises another 15bp
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) weekly observations through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Mortgage rates oscillate with Fed messaging; 25bp weekly swings occur 2-3 times per quarter, creating recurring entry signals.

schema.idea: Mortgage Rate Shock Consumer Stress Fade

data.source: FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) weekly observations through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: When mortgage rates spike 25+ basis points in a single week, consumer discretionary spending typically sees a brief relief bounce the following week as expectations reset lower.

market_impact.rationale: Rate shock paradoxically stabilizes consumer sentiment after volatility capitulation, unlocking pent-up discretionary demand.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly change in MORTGAGE30US exceeds +25bp and closes above prior close, enter long XLY at Friday close
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if mortgage rate rises another 15bp

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Mortgage rates oscillate with Fed messaging; 25bp weekly swings occur 2-3 times per quarter, creating recurring entry signals.

title: Industrial Metals Price Momentum Divergence Snap
thesis: When XLB momentum diverges negatively from copper price momentum over 10 days (copper rises, XLB lags by 2%+), mean reversion in materials leadership typically snaps back within 5-8 trading days. Materials sector catches up to commodity price strength after brief momentum lag, driven by margin expansion expectations.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLB (materials ETF) and spot copper (via price_only adapter) plus FRED industrial production index
signal_logic: If copper outperforms XLB by 2%+ over 10 days and volatility is rising, enter long XLB
entry_exit: Entry: If copper outperforms XLB by 2%+ over 10 days and volatility is rising, enter long XLB Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if divergence widens beyond 3%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLB (materials ETF) and spot copper (via price_only adapter) plus FRED industrial production index via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity and equity momentum divergences are common during risk-on/risk-off cycles; 2%+ divergences occur every 2-3 weeks.

schema.idea: Industrial Metals Price Momentum Divergence Snap

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLB (materials ETF) and spot copper (via price_only adapter) plus FRED industrial production index

schema.behavior: When XLB momentum diverges negatively from copper price momentum over 10 days (copper rises, XLB lags by 2%+), mean reversion in materials leadership typically snaps back within 5-8 trading days.

market_impact.rationale: Materials sector catches up to commodity price strength after brief momentum lag, driven by margin expansion expectations.

trade_logic.entry: If copper outperforms XLB by 2%+ over 10 days and volatility is rising, enter long XLB
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if divergence widens beyond 3%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity and equity momentum divergences are common during risk-on/risk-off cycles; 2%+ divergences occur every 2-3 weeks.

title: Natural Gas Temperature Demand Surprise Reversal
thesis: When weekly heating/cooling degree day anomalies diverge sharply from natural gas consumption trends, mean reversion in energy demand and utility margins typically follows within 5-7 days. Temperature-demand mismatches resolve quickly, creating margin expansion opportunities for utilities as demand normalizes.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily global temperature anomaly plus FRED series GASDESW (natural gas demand) weekly through weather_series + fred_series adapters
signal_logic: If temperature anomaly diverges 1.5+ SD from GASDESW trend, enter long utilities on next Monday
entry_exit: Entry: If temperature anomaly diverges 1.5+ SD from GASDESW trend, enter long utilities on next Monday Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if anomaly reverses
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily global temperature anomaly plus FRED series GASDESW (natural gas demand) weekly through weather_series + fred_series adapters via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature swings and demand volatility create 1.5+ SD divergences 2-3 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Natural Gas Temperature Demand Surprise Reversal

data.source: Open-Meteo daily global temperature anomaly plus FRED series GASDESW (natural gas demand) weekly through weather_series + fred_series adapters

schema.behavior: When weekly heating/cooling degree day anomalies diverge sharply from natural gas consumption trends, mean reversion in energy demand and utility margins typically follows within 5-7 days.

market_impact.rationale: Temperature-demand mismatches resolve quickly, creating margin expansion opportunities for utilities as demand normalizes.

trade_logic.entry: If temperature anomaly diverges 1.5+ SD from GASDESW trend, enter long utilities on next Monday
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if anomaly reverses

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature swings and demand volatility create 1.5+ SD divergences 2-3 times per quarter.

title: Unemployment Initial Claims Mean Reversion Pop
thesis: When initial claims spike 20%+ above their 4-week moving average, they revert within 2-4 weeks; early reversions often precede a bullish labor repricing. Labor market normalization from shock spikes reduces recession fears and supports financial sector re-risking and credit demand.
data_sources: FRED series ICSA (initial jobless claims weekly) through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If ICSA exceeds 4-week MA by 20% or more, enter long on Thursday open
entry_exit: Entry: If ICSA exceeds 4-week MA by 20% or more, enter long on Thursday open Exit: Exit after 14 trading days or if ICSA falls below 4-week MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series ICSA (initial jobless claims weekly) through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Claims data is volatile; 20% spikes above MA occur 3-4 times per year and reverse predictably within 14-21 days.

schema.idea: Unemployment Initial Claims Mean Reversion Pop

data.source: FRED series ICSA (initial jobless claims weekly) through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: When initial claims spike 20%+ above their 4-week moving average, they revert within 2-4 weeks; early reversions often precede a bullish labor repricing.

market_impact.rationale: Labor market normalization from shock spikes reduces recession fears and supports financial sector re-risking and credit demand.

trade_logic.entry: If ICSA exceeds 4-week MA by 20% or more, enter long on Thursday open
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 14 trading days or if ICSA falls below 4-week MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Claims data is volatile; 20% spikes above MA occur 3-4 times per year and reverse predictably within 14-21 days.
